titleStmt.title: SII 23.366: original edition by G.V. Srinivasa Rao

No. 366. (A.R. No. 366 of 1907.) Āḍuturai, Papanasam Taluk, Tanjavur District Āpatsahāyēśvara temple—on the south wall of the maṇḍapa in front of the central shrine Rājak: Kulōttuṅga-Chōḷa (II): year 10: 1145-46 A.D.

summary.p: This is damaged in places. It seems to record an endowment of land after purchase, by one Muppirāl Dakshiṇāmurtti-Bhaṭṭa, making provision, besides the tax due to the ūr for the tirumandirappōnagam offering to the god, for the daily supply of six pots of water (from the river) for the sacred bath and for rearing a flower garden for the supply of flowers. At the end of the record the temple Śivabrahmaṇas agree to burn 4 lamps every day during the services for some gift received from one Pichchadēva-Bhaṭṭa of Araṇaippuṟam another resident of the place. The owner of the land purchased by the donor is said to have been a resident of Vikramaśōḻa-chaturvēdimaṅgalam which is called a Tiruppaḷḷippaḍai (final resting place), obviously of king Vikrama-Chōḷa.

revisionDesc.change: Conversion of digital encoding to DHARMA encoding scheme according to EGD (Encoding Guide for Diplomatic Editions)

div.p:
sva[ stiśrīk] Ovirājakecaripanmarā
ṉa tiripuvaṉaccakkaravattikaḷ śrī kulot
tuṅkacoḻatevaṟkku yāṇṭu pattāvatu Uyya
kkoṇṭārvaḷanāṭṭu tiraimūrnāṭṭu
nāṭṭut tiruppaḷḷipaṭai vikkiramacoḻaccatuppe
timaṅkalattu vetakomapuṟattu keṅkātirapaṭ
ṭarpakkal Aṟutikkūṟu Iṭṭu vilaiyāl mup
pirāl tekṣaṇāmūrttipaṭṭar koṇṭuṭaiya
pākam Uḷḷūr maṉai
ṅkalum tirumantiraponakattukkum tirūmañcaṉat
tukkum tiruppaḷḷittāmattukkum veṇṭuvatukku
m Āka Ūrkku veṇṭuvatu nikki Uḷḷa Uṭalil tiruma
ñcaṉattukku nittam Āṟu kuṭattukku Āṭṭup patinka
la nellum tirunantavanam kuḻi muppatukku ne
llu patinkalamum nikki niṉṟatu tirumantira pona
kamāka viṭṭatu Itu panmāyecuvara rakṣai ....
Ivvūr Araṇaippuṟattu piccatevappaṭṭaṉpakkal Ikko
yilil civappirāmaṇarom Uṭaiyapaṭikoṇṭu cantirāti
ttavaṟ Erikkakaṭavomāṉa cantiviḷakku nālu Ivai pa
nmāyeycuvararakṣai ....
Digital edition of SII 23.366 by automatically converted to DHARMA conventions.
